title: Branches, Integration, and Merge Evidence · Graph Lens: Branch Reference
summary: Integrate a short-lived change while preserving understandable topology and verifying both file content and combined behavior. Planned artifact: Add and verify the git branch reference increment in a branch-and-merge topology lab. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Create, list, rename, copy, delete, and inspect branches as movable references with upstream metadata.
Use blame as a line-origin clue and connect it to surrounding commits, review context, and later movement without assigning fault.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Branch Reference

theory case — Branches main and repair diverge from merge base b00b1e5. repair changes validation while main changes output formatting; predict fast-forward possibility, resulting parents, and post-merge behavior evidence.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology. Apply that move specifically to Branch Reference while producing a branch-and-merge topology lab.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Branches main and repair diverge from merge base b00b1e5. repair changes validation while main changes output formatting; predict fast-forward possibility, resulting parents, and post-merge behavior evidence. Competency probe: Create, list, rename, copy, delete, and inspect branches as movable references with upstream metadata. A defensible solution applies Branch Reference, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a branch-and-merge topology lab.

content.code:
# Worked focus: git-branch-reference — Create, list, rename, copy, delete, and inspect branches as movable references with upstream metadata.
base=$(git merge-base main repair)
printf 'base=%s\n' "$base"
git diff "$base"..repair
git ls-files --unmerged
# Resolve intent, stage, test, then continue.
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
